claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 3 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Blake
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 3 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care

Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some information about your situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions. Child Benefit eligibility is determined separately for each child, so let me start with the basics:

**Do you have a child or children that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you tell me how many children you're asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have two children I'm asking about. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'll help you assess eligibility for both of your children.

Let me start with your first child. **How old is your first child?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: My first child, Alex, is 17 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! Since Alex is 17, I need to ask a few more questions to determine eligibility.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (This would include things like A-levels, Scottish Highers, NVQs up to level 3, or other approved non-advanced education or training courses.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any education or training at the moment. They finished school and aren't doing anything like that right now.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Let me ask about your second child now.

**How old is your second child?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: My second child, Blake, is 3 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information about Blake.

Now I need to ask a few more questions to complete the assessment for both children:

**Does Blake live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Blake lives with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to ask about Alex:

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me too.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. I have a few more important questions to complete the assessment:

**Are you responsible for Alex and Blake?** (This means you're responsible for their care and upbringing, and you pay towards their upkeep - for example, providing food, clothing, or pocket money.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm responsible for both of them. They live with me and I provide for all their needs - food, clothing, everything they need.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Just a couple more questions:

**Are you currently living in the UK, or do you have a qualifying connection to the UK?** (For example, working in the UK or receiving certain UK benefits.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. One final question:

**Is there anyone else also claiming Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake?** (For example, another parent or guardian.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for either of them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me now assess the eligibility for both Alex and Blake based on the Child Benefit rules.

## Assessment Summary

**For Blake (age 3):**
✅ Blake is **eligible** for Child Benefit. Blake is under 16 years old, lives with you, you're responsible for their care, you live in the UK, and no one else is claiming for them.

**For Alex (age 17):**
❌ Alex is **not eligible** for Child Benefit. While Alex is between 16 and 20 years old and lives with you, the rules require that young people in this age group must be in approved education or training (at least 12 hours per week of supervised study). Since Alex is not currently in any education or training, they do not meet the qualifying conditions for Child Benefit.

## What this means

You can claim Child Benefit for Blake, but not for Alex. If Alex were to start approved education or training in the future (such as A-levels, NVQs up to level 3, or similar non-advanced courses), they could potentially become eligible again, and you could then claim for them at that point.

Would you like any further information about claiming Child Benefit for Blake, or do you have any other questions?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Thank you for explaining that to me. That's really helpful - I understand now that I can claim for Blake but not for Alex unless Alex goes back into education. I appreciate you taking the time to go through everything with me.
